entities.name: ādya-saṃkhyā-grahaṇam
entities.cite: The grasp of adya-samkhya, the first number, is the upaya through which the numbers beyond it — genuinely different items — become cognizable

entities.name: saṃkhyāntara-pratipatti
entities.cite: the numbers beyond it become cognizable

entities.name: śabdāntara-śruti
entities.cite: hearing one word opens cognitive access to other words not yet heard

entities.name: śabda-antara-pratipatti
entities.cite: cognitive access to other words not yet heard

entities.name: upāya
entities.cite: The grasp of adya-samkhya, the first number, is the upaya through which the numbers beyond it become cognizable

entities.name: śabda-tattva
entities.cite: all words are variously-manifested configurations of one underlying word-nature (and ultimately of the one sabda-tattva)

entities.name: saṃskāra
entities.cite: mastering some words confers a capacity for the rest, as mastering early numbers confers the number-series

axioms.name: upaya
axioms.cite: The grasp of adya-samkhya, the first number, is the upaya through which the numbers beyond it — genuinely different items — become cognizable

axioms.name: upaya
axioms.cite: hearing one word opens cognitive access to other words not yet heard

axioms.name: hetu
axioms.cite: mastering some words confers a capacity for the rest, as mastering early numbers confers the number-series

axioms.name: vivartamana
axioms.cite: all words are variously-manifested configurations of one underlying word-nature (and ultimately of the one sabda-tattva)

claims.name: upaya
claims.cite: the grasp of the first number is a means to the cognition of further numbers

claims.name: upaya
claims.cite: the hearing of one word a means to the cognition of another

rejected_readings.label: exclusive_arithmetic
rejected_readings.rendering: The means by which one grasps numbers operates only in arithmetic, not in linguistic learning.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts the verse's explicit parallelism. The verse states 'so too' (tathā) for words, establishing that the systematic means applies equally to linguistic and numerical domains. Reading it as number-exclusive denies the verse's central argumentative structure.

rejected_readings.label: independent_competences
rejected_readings.rendering: Word-learning and number-learning are completely independent intellectual capacities with no systematic connection.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts the verse's yathā-tathā (as...so) structure, which establishes an explicit systematic analogy. The commentary emphasizes that 'mastering some words confers a capacity for the rest, as mastering early numbers confers the number-series,' showing they share a unified structure of acquisition. Treating them as isolated denies this systematic unity.

notes: The contract captures the core systematicity doctrine: learning is not atomistic but systematic, with mastery of one element enabling mastery of others through a common principle. The sorts reflect: cognition events (grasp of first number, cognition of others), powers (the means itself, hearing, samskara), and the absolute (sabda-tattva as underlying unity). The contested note mentions ambiguity about scope (systematicity of acquisition vs. phoneme-schema transfer), but both readings rely on the upaya (means) relations captured in axioms 1-2. Axioms 3-4 add the deeper manifestation theory implied by the commentary.
